relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Messiah frees for freedom; do not return to slavery-yokes.
Circumcision as required boundary would empty Messiah’s benefit for these nations-believers.
Taking that route obligates the whole Law.
Seeking justice through Law-regime falls away from grace.
By Spirit and from trust, hope for justice is eagerly awaited.
In Messiah, the power is trust working through love.
They were running well; something hindered truth’s persuasion.
This persuasion is not from the calling One.
A little leaven can alter the whole lump.
Paul trusts they will not ultimately think otherwise.
If Paul still preached boundary-circumcision, the cross would no longer offend.
His frustration burns against those unsettling freedom.
Freedom is not flesh-license; freedom becomes love-service.
The whole Law gathers into loving the neighbor as yourself.
Biting and devouring one another consumes the body.
Walk by Spirit and flesh-desire will not complete itself.
Spirit and flesh-pattern pull in opposed directions.
Being led by Spirit means not being under Law as regime.
Flesh-works are visible where desire becomes misuse and fracture.
Idolatry, sorcery, enmity, strife, jealousy, anger, ambition, and factions reveal the pattern.
Practiced destruction does not inherit Divine reign.
Spirit-fruit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness.
Gentleness and self-control complete the fruit; no Law stands against such things.
Those belonging to Messiah crucify flesh-pattern with its passions and desires.
If Spirit gives life, walk in rhythm with Spirit.
Empty glory, provocation, and envy have no place in the Spirit-walk.

contemplative_note: Galatians 5 is freedom’s test: freedom either becomes love-service through Spirit or collapses into flesh-pattern. The true sign is trust working through love.
